keyMessages: WCEPS tailors research-based products and services to meet unique education needs.
Pathways programs help educators identify a challenge, choose a pathway, and continue learning through partnership.
WCEPS supports schools and districts with educational resources, assessments, professional learning, program evaluations, and content alignment analyses.
proofPoints: WCEPS is described on the Pathways site as a 501(c)(3) charitable nonprofit organization.
The site says WCEPS extends the impact of University of Wisconsin-Madison educational innovations.
brief.layoutRecommendation: Use a structured one-pager with a summary column, key message cards, evidence-backed proof points, and a bottom CTA band.

headlineOptions: Customized support for schools and districts
subheadOptions: Pathways Powered by WCEPS helps education teams chart a course through research-based resources, professional learning, assessments, program.
WCEPS tailors research-based products and services to meet unique education needs.
WCEPS tailors research-based products and services to meet unique education needs.
copy.body: WCEPS tailors research-based products and services to meet unique education needs. For education leaders, this draft focuses on research-based educational supports and service pathways. WCEPS is described on the Pathways site as a 501(c)(3) charitable nonprofit organization.
bullets: WCEPS tailors research-based products and services to meet unique education needs.
Pathways programs help educators identify a challenge, choose a pathway, and continue learning through partnership.
WCEPS supports schools and districts with educational resources, assessments, professional learning, program evaluations, and content alignment.
WCEPS is described on the Pathways site as a 501(c)(3) charitable nonprofit organization.
The site says WCEPS extends the impact of University of Wisconsin-Madison educational innovations.

fittedCopy.headline: Customized support for schools and districts
fittedCopy.deck: Connect schools and districts with nonprofit, research-informed, customized educational support.
proofPoints: Connect teams with nonprofit, research-informed resources.
Connect UW-Madison educational innovations with practical services.
Tailor research-based products and services to meet unique education needs.

compositionTemplate.typographyScale: magazine headline, one deck, short proof rail
compositionTemplate.artPlateTreatment: edge-to-edge editorial art column or band
compositionTemplate.copyPlacement: large type block with compact editorial proof band

designRecipe.hierarchy: readable one-pager hierarchy with a headline/deck column, integrated image field, full-width proof rows, and compact CTA
designRecipe.artDirection: Premium WCEPS editorial split for education leaders, connecting school and district decision-making with UW-Madison research-informed support through warm Wisconsin red accents, natural light, collaborative education imagery, and tailored-pathway visual cues.
designRecipe.placeholderStrategy: Use crop, light, and directional forms to create readable quiet zones and proof-row rhythm; imply pathways through environmental composition and subtle linework, not fake text, blank cards, charts, or UI modules.
designRecipe.appComposition: official logo, exact headline, deck, proof rows, and CTA are app-rendered in readable zones; art stays secondary to message clarity
promptDirectives: editorial split one-pager with readable proof-row rhythm
avoid dark poster overlays behind long copy
create visual energy in the image field without fake charts, document modules, or text

compositionScore.question: Would I send this to the WCEPS team without apology?

layoutQa.question: Would I send this layout to the WCEPS team without apology?

copyQualityQa.question: Would I send this copy to the WCEPS team without apology?
warnings: Generated copy repeats the same source idea across body, subhead, or bullets before copy-fit.

failureModes.message: Generated copy repeats the same source idea across body, subhead, or bullets before copy-fit.

visualQa.question: Would I send this visual system to the WCEPS team without apology?

renderQa.question: Would I send this rendered artifact to the WCEPS team without apology?

modelQa.question: Would a senior WCEPS reviewer send this artifact without apology?
warnings: Final deck and proof rows repeat similar connect/research-informed/resources/services phrasing, reducing polish.
Copy remains broad and does not translate WCEPS pathways into concrete decision value for education leaders.
Art plate is usable and text-free, but the meeting scene could represent any professional office; school/district context is only lightly implied.
QA reports a WCEPS Pathways logo variant for a parent WCEPS request; verify brand selection before export.
High QA scores overstate approval readiness given generic copy/art, no CTA detail, and unverified final overlay contrast.

safeZones: Create a portrait art field with enough texture to support editorial layout.
Keep important visual energy away from trim and crop edges.
Avoid document panels, empty modules, fake cards, or CTA-like shapes.
exactTextPriority: Customized support for schools and districts
Connect schools and districts with nonprofit, research-informed, customized educational support.
Connect teams with nonprofit, research-informed resources.
Connect UW-Madison educational innovations with practical services.
Tailor research-based products and services to meet unique education needs.
appOwnedElements: Official WCEPS logo from approved asset.
Deterministic grid, typography, headline, audience, and CTA.
Exact support copy and proof hierarchy.
Final export wrapper, source evidence, and review state.
imageGenOwnedElements: Atmosphere, photography or illustration treatment, color, texture, and crop rhythm.
No document layout, fake cards, panels, contact bars, charts, or text.

imageResults.revisedPrompt: Portrait full-bleed campaign art plate, 1024x1536, premium editorial institutional design comp for WCEPS parent brand, education leaders audience. Text-free visual only, no logos, no letters, no numbers, no readable markings. Composition: editorial split one-pager art foundation with a calm, type-safe left column/quiet zone for future app-rendered headline and deck, and a richer right-field visual zone with layered depth. Subject: authentic inclusive group of educators and district leaders in a bright school district office or professional learning planning space, reviewing non-readable resource packets, planning notes, and strategy materials; research-informed educational support and service pathways implied through environment and interaction, not literal charts. Warm institutional nonprofit education tone, credible human collaboration, practical and clear. Use WCEPS-adjacent palette atmosphere: deep blue-green shadows (#142836), teal and aqua accents (#0081A4, #3EB3BD), soft green natural accents (#5EA974), bright daylight, clean surfaces, subtle depth-of-field. Visual rhythm: right-side active collaboration scene with diagonal light, shelves, glass, whiteboard-like surfaces only as abstract texture with no writing; lower area includes subtle full-width proof-row rhythm implied through repeated environmental bands/material edges but not panels or cards. Compact CTA-safe quiet area near lower left/center without button shapes or labels. Avoid dark poster overlays behind copy; avoid blank boxes, document layouts, fake cards, UI, charts, icons, badges, seals, chevrons, arrows, mountain marks, logos, text, close-up faces, detailed hands, graduation imagery, diplomas, mascot, generic classroom stock, posed workshop look. Keep important visual energy away from trim edges; polished NY media/ad-agency quality; refined editorial photography with slight abstract-illustrative polish, rich visual density but readable quiet zones.

request.topic: research-based educational supports and service pathways
request.notes: Use the parent brand without over-describing individual programs.

sourceEvidence.excerpt: Discover your pathway through WCEPS’ research-based educational resources, publications, assessments, professional learning, program evaluations, and content alignment analyses.
sourceEvidence.label: Homepage positioning

sourceEvidence.excerpt: Discover our Pathways programs and see how they can support you and your school in achieving your goals.
sourceEvidence.label: Pathways comparison entry

sourceEvidence.excerpt: Pathways listings describe overview, who we help, and area of need across districts, schools, education leaders, teachers, higher education, and content developers.
sourceEvidence.label: Service filters for leaders

sourceEvidence.excerpt: WCEPS is a non-profit organization meeting a growing demand for bringing education research and development to educators around the world.
sourceEvidence.label: Research and development basis
